rewrite this in very formal language:
Python is one of the famous programming languages in the fields of data science and big
data for its simple and readable syntax and the high availability of machine learning and
deep learning packages. These packages were developed and maintained by experts in
the data science field to make it easier to develop machine learning models for users. The
models implemented in this paper were built and evaluated using python libraries such
as Pytorch, Pandas and matplot

Python is renowned as a prominent programming language within the realms of data science and big data, largely due to its simplistic and legible syntax as well as the vast array of machine learning and deep learning libraries at its disposal. These libraries have been diligently crafted and sustained by distinguished experts in the field of data science, with the express purpose of facilitating a more seamless experience in constructing machine learning models for the end-users. The models featured within this scholarly article were meticulously constructed and appraised utilizing Python libraries, including, but not limited to, Pytorch, Pandas, and Matplotlib.

rewrite this in very formal language:
Pytorch is an open-source machine learning library developed by Facebook’s artificial
intelligence research group. The pytorch library has the advantage over other machine
learning libraries because it uses dynamic computation which offers high flexibility in
building complex deep learning models. The pytorch library was used to initiated the
classes of the models, load the data entries for the model and develop the training function.
Also the dropout package was used to implement the dropout algorithm in the models

Pytorch, an open-source machine learning library, has been ingeniously developed by Facebook's distinguished artificial intelligence research collective. This particular library holds a pronounced edge over its contemporaries in the realm of machine learning due to its employment of dynamic computation, thus providing a heightened degree of flexibility in the architectural design of intricate deep learning models. The Pytorch library has been duly utilized in this context to effectuate the initiation of the model classes, facilitate the data entry loading for the model, and devise the training function accordingly. Moreover, the incorporation of the dropout package warrants the successful implementation of the dropout algorithm within the models in question.

rewrite this in very formal language:
Pandas is a specialized python library for data analysis especially on humongous datasets. The
functionality of Pandas library includes the ability of reading and writing data and
reshaping the dataset. Pandas is also capable of re-ordering and cleaning time series data
for time series analysis. The Pandas library was used to read the csv file of the dataset and
creating the data array that can be read by the model.

Pandas constitutes a highly specific Python library, designed explicitly for the analysis of colossal datasets. Among its numerous functionalities, Pandas encompasses the capacity for reading and composing data, reshaping datasets, and meticulously reorganizing and cleansing time series data to facilitate time series analysis. Within the context of this particular endeavor, the Pandas library has been employed to read the dataset's CSV file and subsequently generate a data array that the model is able to interpret effectively.
write about keras library in python in very formal language

Keras is a sophisticated Python library, purposefully crafted to facilitate the development and experimentation of deep learning models with unparalleled efficacy. Serving as a user-friendly, modular, and easily extendable interface, Keras seamlessly integrates with lower-level, robust deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow and Theano. The library encompasses a host of state-of-the-art, pre-built modules for common neural network components such as layers, optimizers, activation functions, and various other utilities, thereby enabling researchers and practitioners to expeditiously prototype, construct, and validate their models without the necessity of developing these components from scratch. Consequently, Keras has garnered widespread acclaim and adoption from both the academic and industry sectors of the global artificial intelligence and deep learning communities.
write about tensorflow library in python in very formal language

TensorFlow is a comprehensive and versatile library written in Python, specifically tailored for the development and execution of sophisticated machine learning and deep learning algorithms. Originating from the eminent research team at Google Brain, TensorFlow offers a robust, flexible, and efficient platform that enables both novice and experienced users to design, train, and deploy intricate mathematical computations with the utmost ease and accuracy.

The library extensively incorporates data flow graphs, whereby nodes represent mathematical operations, and edges signify multidimensional arrays (tensors) in communication between said nodes. This architectural structure allows TensorFlow to seamlessly embody an inherently parallel and distributed structure, thus ensuring optimal performance and scalability on a wide array of computational devices, ranging from handheld mobile devices to high-performance computing clusters.

Furthermore, TensorFlow's compatibility with an extensive assortment of tools and libraries, such as Keras and TensorBoard, serves to enrich the user experience by offering advanced functionalities and visualization capabilities. As a result, TensorFlow maintains its reputation as a preeminent choice for both industry and academic practitioners, championing numerous advancements in the fields of artificial intelligence, deep learning, and machine learning.

rewrite this in very formal language:
The purpose of this layer is to receive a feature map. Usually, we start with
low number of filters for low-level feature detection. The deeper we go into
the CNN, the more filters we use to detect high-level features. Feature
detection is based on ‘scanning’ the input with the filter of a given size and
applying matrix computations in order to derive a feature map.

The primary objective of this layer is to obtain a feature map, which is essential for the functioning of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). Typically, the process commences with a reduced quantity of filters, thereby enabling the detection of low-level features. As the system delves deeper into the CNN, the number of filters employed correspondingly increases, facilitating the discernment of higher-level features. The detection of these features is predicated on the methodical examination of the input, employing filters of predetermined dimensions and engaging in intricate matrix computations for the ultimate derivation of the feature map.

rewrite this in very formal language:
The goal of this layer is to provide spatial variance, which simply means that
the system will be capable of recognizing an object even when its
appearance varies in some way. Pooling layer will perform a downsampling
operation along the spatial dimensions (width, height), resulting in output
such as [16x16x12] for pooling_size=(2, 2).

The primary objective of this layer is to confer spatial invariance, essentially ensuring that the system is adept at identifying an object notwithstanding any alteration in its presentation. This pooling layer subsequently executes a downsampling procedure along the spatial dimensions, namely width and height. Consequently, for a pooling_size parameter of (2, 2), the resultant output emerges as a dimensional array of [16x16x12].

rewrite this in very formal language:
In a fully connected layer, we flatten the output of the last convolution layer
and connect every node of the current layer with the other nodes of the next
layer. Neurons in a fully connected layer have full connections to all
activations in the previous layer, as seen in regular Neural Networks and
work in a similar way.

Within a fully connected layer, the output derived from the terminal convolution layer is flattened, subsequently establishing a comprehensive connection between each node belonging to the current layer and all corresponding nodes of the subsequent layer. The neurons present in the fully connected layer boast an extensive connection with every activation in the preceding layer, analogous to the composition of conventional Neural Networks, and display a similar mode of operation.

rewrite this in very formal language:
The complete image classification pipeline can be formalized as follows:
Our input is a training dataset that consists of N images, each labeled
with one of 2 different classes.
Then, we use this training set to train a classifier to learn what every
one of the classes looks like.
In the end, we evaluate the quality of the classifier by asking it to
predict labels for a new set of images that it has never seen before. We
will then compare the true labels of these images to the ones
predicted by the classifier.

The comprehensive image classification pipeline can be encapsulated through the subsequent framework. The initial input entails a training dataset comprising N distinct images, each assigned with a label corresponding to one of two unique classes. Subsequently, this training set is employed to instruct a classifier in discerning the appearance of every individual class. The final stage involves evaluating the classifier's performance by tasking it with predicting labels for an entirely new assortment of images with which it has no prior familiarity. The authenticity of these images' labels will then be contrasted with the predictions proffered by the classifier, thereby ascertaining its overall efficacy.

rewrite this in very formal language:
I used a batch size value of 16. Batch size is one of the most important
hyperparameters to tune in deep learning. I prefer to use a larger batch size
to train my models as it allows computational speedups from the parallelism of GPUs. However, it is well known that too large of a batch size will lead to
poor generalization. On the one extreme, using a batch equal to the entire
dataset guarantees convergence to the global optima of the objective
function. However this is at the cost of slower convergence to that optima.
On the other hand, using smaller batch sizes have been shown to have faster
convergence to good results. This is intuitively explained by the fact that
smaller batch sizes allow the model to start learning before having to see all
the data. The downside of using a smaller batch size is that the model is not
guaranteed to converge to the global optima.Therefore it is often advised that
one starts at a small batch size reaping the benefits of faster training
dynamics and steadily grows the batch size through training. I also did some data augmentation. The practice of data augmentation is an
effective way to increase the size of the training set. Augmenting the training
examples allow the network to see more diversified, but still representative
data points during training.
Then I created a data generator to get the data from our folders and into
Keras in an automated way. Keras provides convenient python generator
functions for this purpose.

A batch size value of 16 was employed in this scenario. The batch size parameter constitutes a crucial hyperparameter warranting optimization within the realm of deep learning. Personally, I advocate for the utilization of a larger batch size during model training, as it capitalizes on the parallelism offered by GPUs, thus enhancing computational expediency. Nevertheless, it remains widely acknowledged that an excessively large batch size may result in suboptimal generalization. For instance, employing a batch size equivalent to the entirety of the dataset ensures convergence to the global optima of the objective function, albeit at the expense of a more protracted convergence process. Conversely, smaller batch sizes tend to demonstrate accelerated convergence towards favorable outcomes. Intuitively, this phenomenon arises from the capacity of smaller batch sizes to initiate the learning process without necessitating exposure to the complete dataset. However, such an approach fails to guarantee convergence to the global optima. Consequently, it is often advised that practitioners commence with smaller batch sizes, reaping the advantages of swifter training dynamics, while gradually increasing the batch size throughout the training process.

Additionally, certain data augmentation techniques were executed as well. Data augmentation is widely recognized as an effective method of expanding the size of the training set, wherein the augmentation of training examples allows the neural network to encounter more diversified, albeit still representative, data points during the training phase.

Subsequently, a data generator was established to ensure the seamless transfer of datasets from their designated folders into the Keras framework in an automated fashion. Keras conveniently furnishes Python generator functions tailored for this specific purpose.
